title: Igroup Presence Questionnaire (IPQ)
description: 14-item self-report scale measuring sense of presence in virtual environments. Developed by Schubert, Friedmann & Regenbrecht (2001).
citation: Schubert, T., Friedmann, F., & Regenbrecht, H. (2001). The experience of presence: Factor analytic insights. Presence, 10(3), 266-281.

General_Presence.description: Single global item: sense of being surrounded by the VE

items.text: Somehow I felt that the virtual world surrounded me.

Spatial_Presence.description: Sense of physically being located inside the VE

items.text: I felt like I was just perceiving pictures.
I had a sense of acting in the virtual space, rather than operating something from outside.
I felt present in the virtual space.
I did not feel present in the virtual space.
I felt like I was in the virtual space.

Involvement.description: Attentional engagement directed toward the VE; real-world unawareness

items.text: I noticed the virtual environment through more than one sense.
I had the impression that the virtual environment was something I was part of.
I was not aware of my real environment.
I concentrated only on the virtual space.

Realness.description: Perceived authenticity and realism of the VE relative to the real world

items.text: The virtual world seemed more realistic to me than the real world.
The virtual environment appeared real to me.
The experience in the virtual environment felt consistent with my real world experience.
My experience in the virtual environment was comparable to the real world.

scoring.method: Mean of items per subscale after reverse-scoring marked items
scoring.subscale_scores: Computed separately; no single composite total is standard
scoring.factor_structure: Three second-order factors confirmed via CFA

intended_use: Immersive VR, desktop VR, 3D games, text-based VEs
known_limitations: Item overlap with SUS and PQ
Does not capture social presence or negative effects
